name: Arrow Dynamics
notes: Pioneer of the modern steel roller coaster. Invented the tubular steel track, corkscrew, suspended coaster, and stand-up coaster.

name: Bolliger & Mabillard
notes: Known for extremely smooth, reliable coasters. Responsible for Kings Island's Diamondback (hyper), Banshee (inverted), and Orion (giga).

name: Premier Rides
notes: Specializes in linear induction motor (LIM) launched coasters. Built both Flight of Fear and the former Italian Job/Backlot Stunt Coaster at Kings Island.

name: Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.
notes: One of the oldest coaster manufacturers in the world. Built the Racer and Woodstock Express at Kings Island, both designed by the legendary John C. Allen.

name: Vekoma
notes: Dutch manufacturer responsible for Invertigo, Woodstock's Air Rail, Snoopy's Soap Box Racers, and the former Firehawk at Kings Island.

name: Great Coasters International
notes: Sunbury, Pennsylvania-based wooden coaster specialist. Built Mystic Timbers (2017) and also performs re-tracking work on other manufacturers' coasters.

name: Kings Island
notes: The park designed and built The Beast in-house. It remains the world's longest wooden roller coaster.

name: TOGO
notes: Japanese manufacturer best known for stand-up coasters. Built King Cobra (1984), the world's first modern stand-up roller coaster.

name: E&F Miler Industries
notes: Family-owned Portland, Oregon firm founded by Carl Miler in the late 1940s. Relaunched by his son Fred Miler in 1989 and renamed E&F Miler Industries in 2013 when Eric Miler joined. Built the Great Pumpkin Coaster at Kings Island in 1992.

name: Roller Coaster Corporation of America
notes: Atlanta-based wooden coaster builder founded by Michael and Stephen Black. Built Son of Beast (2000), which was the world's tallest, fastest, and first looping wooden coaster. A lawsuit with Kings Island over construction quality followed the ride's deterioration. Ceased operations around 2005.

name: S.D.C.
notes: Italian manufacturer founded by Walter Spaggiari, Mack D. Duce, and Giancarlo Casoli. Best known for the mass-produced portable Galaxi model coaster, of which over 50 were built worldwide. Filed for bankruptcy in 1993. The Bavarian Beetle at Kings Island was a Galaxi model.

name: Caripro
notes: Small Dutch manufacturer based in Limburg, founded by Hans Gubbels (former Vekoma employee). Built 9 coasters, primarily the Batflyer suspended family model. Acquired by Vekoma in 2000–2001 and liquidated shortly after. The former Scooby's Ghoster Coaster at Kings Island was a Batflyer.
